Patch Set 17: (9 inline comments)

Patch Set 17:

I didn't think you would be adding as single {GET,SET}_INFO key and then further multiplexing sub-commands into a single key. I thought there might be one or two GET_INFO or SET_INFO RPCs in total. The current code is just getting very messy in the network protocol. With unified targets it should be easy to add a new RPC opcode that is handled by both OSS and MDS nodes.
Patch Set 17: (1 inline comment)
Patch Set 17: (1 inline comment)

Patch Set 17:

It would be cool if LFSCK is a service on all the targets (including MDT and OST), so that LFSCK talks to LFSCK using own RPC set (LFSCK control protocol ) and uses OSD API to access storage.

Uploaded patch set 30.

Patch Set 30: Code-Review-1

I'm not sure why we need to put all these bits into MDT/OFD. can you clarify on this?

Patch Set 30:

Build Started http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/21067/

Patch Set 30: Verified+1

Build Successful 
 
http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/21067/ : SUCCESS

Patch Set 30:

Rosso Autotest has started the test process

Patch Set 30:

Rosso Autotest has started the test process

Patch Set 30:

Rosso Autotest has started the test process

Patch Set 30:

Tests received by maloo, run on CentOS release 6.4/x86_64: (https://maloo.whamcloud.com/test_sessions/632cb8b2-7e29-11e3-91f7-52540035b04c).  Ran 6 tests.  No failures.

Patch Set 30:

The user space LFSCK command via l_ioctl() down to lustre and processed by the MDT/OFD as other user space tools do. The LFSCK RPC handlers are in MDT/OFD, the same as other RPCs. So I did not catch your questions…

Patch Set 30:

so, if LFSCK is represented with own OBD, then there will be much less need to puts LFSCK bits in MDT/OFD ?

Patch Set 30:

Yes, if LFSCK has its own OBD, then related processing can be migrated to its own interfaces.

But consider old user-space LFSCK tools, we already used MDT/OFD for such purpose. We need to do more work to handle the interoperability issues if we move them into LFSCK OBD.

On the other hand, the LFSCK pause/auto-restart are all controlled by MDT/OFD, it is normal to handle manually LFSCK start/stop at the same places.

Patch Set 30:

what kind of compatibility issues?

Patch Set 30:

old tools use "lctl lfsck_start -M lustre-MDT0000"

Patch Set 30:

it's just matter of naming - lctl itself can append a suffix (say, "-lfsck") and that's all.

Patch Set 30:

If we ignore the possible users own existing LFSCK tools to use fixed "MDTxxxx", then it is OK.

What about the notification from LFSCK to MDT/OFD (last_id rebuilding/rebuilt)?

Any potential trouble when new LFSCK OBD downgrade to old lustre-2.4/5?

What's benefit with new LFSCK OBD for so much changes? If we decide to change it, then all MDT/OFD related patches in the LFSCK patch series will be affected. I am afraid that there is not enough time for that.

On the other hand, it is historical using the MDT/OFD for LFSCK handle interface even without current patch applied. So is it possible to resolve it in other ticket if we want to do that?

Patch Set 30:

the benefit is to separate functionality and keep away from "all-in-one" approach. if there is no strong need to put things together why would we do so? it's easier to develop, test, inspect and maintain if the bits are separated logically. otherwise we have current situation where lots of patches touch lots of files, introducing false dependencies, making it hard to inspect.

as for lastid I tend to think ad-hook approach isn't very nice and some more normal options (like remount) should be investigated. 

clearly it should be possible to fix later, but (sorry) - why didn't we try to make it right previously, this is not the first time we discuss structuring, right? why we still have easy-to-fix debts from 1.5 blocking new development?

Patch Set 30:

We have discussed similar topics at LFSCK 1.5 period when lustre-2.4. At that time, the conclusion was that be as LFSCK 1.5 technical debts, separate the LFSCK code from MDD into LFSCK module, but there was no requirement for LFSCK OBD. So when I reorganised the LFSCK code in lustre-2.5, no LFSCK OBD was introduced, then the technical debts patches have been submitted to lustre-2.5...

Patch Set 30:

I've been trying to remind about UT in all appropriate and inappropriate cases. LFSCK should be a separate module capable to run on MDT, OST and mixed/unified one. the more it structured this way now, the less work for you to adopt to UT and for Mike on his way to UT.

Patch Set 30:

Alex: sorry, I did not aware such issues during Lustre-2.5 period, so your planed things were not implemented at that time. Currently, changing all related things together with this patch will affect the 2.6 feature frozen. So I prefer to open new Jira ticket and process it as technical debts later. How do you think?

Patch Set 30: Code-Review+1

(3 comments)

I think it makes sense to investigate possibility of turning LFSCK in a separate service with own ODB representation. Ideally it would be possible to run LFSCK with no main services like MDT/OST.

parents.subject: LU-1267 lfsck: enhance RPCs (1) for MDT-OST consistency

commit.subject: LU-1267 lfsck: enhance RPCs (2) for MDT-OST consistency
commit.message:
LU-1267 lfsck: enhance RPCs (2) for MDT-OST consistency

The LFSCK on the MDT uses LFSCK_NOTIFY RPC to control the LFSCK
on the OSTs (or other MDTs) to start/stop/fail/pause the layout
consistency verification. And uses LFSCK_QUERY RPC to query the
LFSCK status on the OSTs (or other MDTs).

Introduce new connection flag: OBD_CONNECT_LFSCK to indicate that
whether the target server (MDT/OST) supports online LFSCK or not.
